name: Leucovorin (folinic acid)
mechanism_summary: Reduced folate that bypasses folate-receptor autoantibody blockade; restores cerebral folate transport. Crosses BBB.
notes: Calibration target: CSRS > 80

name: Sulforaphane (broccoli sprout extract)
mechanism_summary: Activates NRF2 antioxidant response; upregulates phase II detox enzymes; reduces oxidative stress associated with autism phenotypes.
notes: Singh 2014 RCT in adolescent males
targets_legacy: NRF2 pathway; oxidative stress; HSF1; mitochondrial function

name: Methyl-B12 (methylcobalamin) injection
mechanism_summary: Provides active form of B12 to support methylation; subcutaneous injection bypasses GI absorption issues common in autism.
notes: Hendren 2016 RCT

name: N-Acetylcysteine (NAC)
mechanism_summary: Replenishes glutathione; modulates glutamatergic neurotransmission; documented effect on irritability in ASD trials.
notes: Hardan 2012 RCT for irritability
targets_legacy: Glutathione precursor; glutamate modulation; oxidative stress

name: Bumetanide
mechanism_summary: Loop diuretic that reduces intracellular chloride in immature neurons, restoring GABA's inhibitory polarity. Phase 2 positive; phase 3 mixed.
notes: Lemonnier 2017 phase 2 positive

name: Low-dose naltrexone (LDN)
mechanism_summary: Sub-clinical doses produce paradoxical immunomodulation; reduces neuroinflammation; case reports in ASD.
notes: Limited RCT data; broader off-label use

name: Ketogenic diet
mechanism_summary: Shifts brain energy metabolism to ketones; modulates mitochondrial function and GABA tone; small open-label autism trials positive.
notes: Lee 2018 small open-label

name: Methylated folate (5-MTHF) — preconception/prenatal
mechanism_summary: Bioavailable form of folate that bypasses MTHFR variants; preconception/prenatal supplementation associated with reduced autism risk in some cohorts.
notes: Surén 2013 MoBa cohort
targets_legacy: MTHFR; methylation cycle; neural tube development

name: Reverse osmosis water filtration
mechanism_summary: Removes >99% of PFAS/glyphosate/heavy metals from drinking water; reduces prenatal and childhood exposure to known endocrine disruptors and neurotoxicants.
notes: See Miami Beach PFAS memo for documented exposure pathway

name: Leucovorin + methyl-B12 + sulforaphane (combo)
mechanism_summary: Combination targets cerebral folate deficiency, methylation cycle dysfunction, and oxidative stress simultaneously. Theoretical combo, not yet RCT-tested as a triple.
notes: First-class combo example

name: L-carnitine
mechanism_summary: Carnitine deficiency in autism subset; supplementation restores mitochondrial fatty acid oxidation.

name: Coenzyme Q10 (ubiquinol)
mechanism_summary: Mitochondrial cofactor; ubiquinol form preferred for absorption.

name: Vitamin D3
mechanism_summary: Maternal/postnatal vitamin D deficiency associated with autism risk; supplementation in deficient children improves behaviors.

name: Omega-3 (EPA + DHA)
mechanism_summary: DHA critical for neurodevelopment; preconception/prenatal omega-3 reduces risk; postnatal modestly benefits behaviors.

name: Magnesium glycinate
mechanism_summary: Highly bioavailable form; calming effect on hyperactivity.

name: Zinc (zinc picolinate)
mechanism_summary: Zinc deficiency / elevated copper:zinc ratio in autism subset; supplementation normalizes ratio.

name: Pyridoxal-5-phosphate (active B6)
mechanism_summary: Active B6; cofactor for serotonin, GABA, dopamine; classic Rimland B6+Mg protocol.

name: Trimethylglycine (TMG / betaine)
mechanism_summary: Methyl donor for homocysteine remethylation.

name: S-adenosylmethionine (SAMe)
mechanism_summary: Universal methyl donor for low-SAMe phenotypes.

name: Glutathione (liposomal or precursors)
mechanism_summary: Master antioxidant; depletion documented in autism.

name: Lithium orotate (low-dose)
mechanism_summary: Sub-clinical lithium modulates GSK-3beta; some open-label autism reports.

name: Inositol (myo-inositol)
mechanism_summary: Involved in serotonin/dopamine signaling; small studies in OCD and autism repetitive behaviors.

name: Taurine
mechanism_summary: Inhibitory neuromodulator; supports GABA tone.

name: Glycine
mechanism_summary: Improves sleep; precursor to glutathione.

name: Probiotics (multi-strain)
mechanism_summary: Specific strains (Visbiome) modulate autism gut microbiome.

name: Berberine
mechanism_summary: Activates AMPK; modulates microbiome; emerging interest.

name: Curcumin (high-bioavailability)
mechanism_summary: Crosses BBB in bioavailable forms; modulates microglial activation.

name: Resveratrol
mechanism_summary: SIRT1 activator; mitochondrial biogenesis.

name: Quercetin
mechanism_summary: Mast cell stabilizer; relevant for autism + MCAS subset.

name: Choline (CDP-choline / Alpha-GPC)
mechanism_summary: Methyl donor; preconception/prenatal choline reduces neurodevelopmental risk.

name: Bacopa monnieri
mechanism_summary: Adaptogen; pediatric ADHD trials show cognitive improvement.

name: Ashwagandha
mechanism_summary: Adaptogen; reduces cortisol and anxiety.

name: Lion's mane (Hericium erinaceus)
mechanism_summary: Stimulates NGF synthesis; mechanistic plausibility for neurodevelopment.

name: Saffron
mechanism_summary: RCTs show comparable efficacy to methylphenidate in pediatric ADHD.

name: Cannabidiol (CBD)
mechanism_summary: Multiple small autism trials show behavior and seizure improvements.

name: Rapamycin (sirolimus)
mechanism_summary: FDA-approved for TSC; off-label in autism + mTOR variants.

name: Metformin
mechanism_summary: Activates AMPK; modulates microbiome.

name: Memantine
mechanism_summary: NMDA modulator; mixed RCT results.

name: Riluzole
mechanism_summary: Glutamate modulator; FDA for ALS; small autism trials.

name: D-cycloserine
mechanism_summary: NMDA partial agonist; small trials for autism social cognition.

name: GFCF diet
mechanism_summary: Eliminates gluten and dairy; mixed RCT results overall but responsive subset.

name: GAPS diet
mechanism_summary: Multi-stage healing diet; anecdotal autism community endorsement.

name: Low-glutamate diet
mechanism_summary: Reduces excitatory load; small case series.

name: Cruciferous vegetable-rich diet
mechanism_summary: Whole-food source of sulforaphane and indole-3-carbinol.

name: Low-oxalate diet
mechanism_summary: Eliminates high-oxalate foods; controversial.

name: Sleep architecture optimization + melatonin
mechanism_summary: Sleep disturbance prevalent; structured sleep hygiene + melatonin (0.5-3 mg) improves daytime function.

name: Aerobic exercise (structured)
mechanism_summary: Increases BDNF, reduces neuroinflammation; small autism RCTs positive.

name: Sauna / heat therapy
mechanism_summary: Activates HSPs; supports detoxification.

name: Sunlight exposure
mechanism_summary: Vitamin D synthesis + circadian entrainment.

name: EMF reduction (bedroom)
mechanism_summary: Removes wifi/cell from bedroom; mechanism via oxidative stress and circadian.

name: HEPA air filtration
mechanism_summary: Reduces airborne pollutants; relevant given air-pollution-autism associations.

name: Glass / stainless replacement of plastic
mechanism_summary: Removes major exposure pathway for endocrine disruptors.

name: Organic produce
mechanism_summary: Reduces glyphosate and pesticide exposure.

name: Mold remediation
mechanism_summary: Hidden mold linked to immune dysregulation.

name: Mitochondrial cocktail (CoQ10+L-carnitine+B-complex)
mechanism_summary: Standard mitochondrial cocktail; targets multiple complexes.

name: Gut healing combo (GFCF + L-glutamine + probiotics)
mechanism_summary: Targets gut-brain axis comprehensively.

name: Anti-inflammatory combo (sulforaphane + omega-3 + vitamin D)
mechanism_summary: Three independent anti-inflammatory mechanisms.

name: Preconception methylation panel + targeted nutrient repletion
mechanism_summary: Test parental methylation status; replete deficiencies before conception.

name: Vaginal microbiome seeding at C-section
mechanism_summary: Vaginal swab transfer to C-section infant; controversial.

name: Extended exclusive breastfeeding (>=6mo)
mechanism_summary: Human milk oligosaccharides shape infant microbiome.

name: Intranasal oxytocin
mechanism_summary: Synthetic oxytocin delivered intranasally; modulates social cognition via amygdala/PFC. Acute and chronic dosing studied.
notes: Parker 2017 RCT positive in subset.

name: BPC-157 (peptide)
mechanism_summary: Pentadecapeptide derived from gastric BPC; preclinical anti-inflammatory and gut-protective effects. No autism RCT.
notes: Speculative; preclinical strong, clinical absent in autism.

name: Selank (peptide)
mechanism_summary: Synthetic analog of tuftsin; anxiolytic and nootropic in animal models. Russian human studies show anxiolytic effect.
notes: Speculative.

name: Semax (peptide)
mechanism_summary: ACTH(4-10) analog; nootropic and BDNF-promoting in animal models. No autism trials.
notes: Speculative.

name: Cerebrolysin
mechanism_summary: Porcine brain-derived peptide mixture; trophic effects via neurotrophins. Used off-label in some pediatric autism protocols (Eastern Europe).
notes: Pediatric autism trials small; off-label use widespread outside US.

name: Iodine (potassium iodide / kelp)
mechanism_summary: Iodine repletion to support thyroid hormone synthesis during pregnancy and lactation; preventable cause of neurodevelopmental impairment.
notes: WHO recommends universal salt iodization.

name: Selenium (selenomethionine)
mechanism_summary: Cofactor for glutathione peroxidase; antioxidant support and thyroid hormone deiodination.
notes: Narrow therapeutic window; high doses toxic.

name: NAD+ precursors (NMN / NR)
mechanism_summary: Nicotinamide mononucleotide / riboside; raise cellular NAD+. Activate sirtuins, support mitochondrial function. No autism RCT; promising aging biomarkers.
notes: Emerging area. Regulatory note: FDA reclassified NMN as drug for NDI purposes (2022); NR remains as supplement.

name: Pregnenolone (low-dose)
mechanism_summary: Master neurosteroid; precursor for cortisol, DHEA, allopregnanolone. Small studies in autism for irritability.
notes: Hormone — caution in children.

name: Levothyroxine (T4) replacement
mechanism_summary: Standard thyroid hormone replacement; restores fetal/childhood thyroid hormone in confirmed hypothyroidism.
notes: Only when hypothyroidism documented.

name: Vagal nerve stimulation (transcutaneous)
mechanism_summary: tVNS via auricular branch; activates cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway, modulates gut-brain axis.
notes: Devices like Nurosym widely available.

name: Screen time reduction (structured)
mechanism_summary: Limiting screen exposure in 0-3y per AAP guidelines; preserve social interaction windows.
notes: Free; low risk.

name: Cold exposure / cold plunge
mechanism_summary: Brief cold immersion increases norepinephrine, dopamine, vagal tone; emerging interest for behavioral regulation.
notes: Anecdotal in autism; mainstream cold exposure literature supports HRV/mood benefits.

name: Breathwork / vagal toning practices
mechanism_summary: Slow diaphragmatic breathing, box breathing, humming; raises HRV and parasympathetic tone. Polyvagal-informed therapy.
notes: Free; safe.

name: MBSR / stress reduction (parent + child)
mechanism_summary: Mindfulness-based stress reduction for both parents and children with autism; reduces cortisol, improves family dynamics.
notes: Indirect benefit on child via parent regulation.

name: Fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT)
mechanism_summary: Whole microbiota transfer from healthy donor (often familial). Kang 2017 open-label MTT showed sustained 45% reduction in ASD symptoms 2 years post; Hazan 2024 familial sibling FMT case report showed nonverbal → verbal emergence with Bifidobacterium restoration.
dose_range: Single 300 mL infusion or multi-dose over 8 weeks
notes: Kang 2017+2019 sustained outcomes; Hazan 2024.

name: Bifidobacterium infantis / longum probiotic
mechanism_summary: Strain-specific probiotic targeting documented Bifidobacterium depletion in autism. B. infantis 35624 (Align) and B. longum BB536 most-studied.
notes: More targeted than multi-strain INT-0025.

name: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG
mechanism_summary: Most-studied probiotic strain; produces GABA, modulates immune tone, reduces intestinal permeability. Some autism RCTs positive.
notes: Parracho 2010, Santocchi 2020.

name: Saccharomyces boulardii (yeast probiotic)
mechanism_summary: Non-colonizing yeast probiotic; antagonist to Candida and C. difficile. Used in functional autism protocols for fungal dysbiosis.
notes: Parker 2018.

name: Akkermansia muciniphila (live)
mechanism_summary: Mucin-degrading commensal that paradoxically thickens the mucin layer; modulates metabolism and BBB integrity. Pasteurized form FDA novel-food approved.
notes: Newly available; emerging interest.

name: Human milk oligosaccharides (HMOs)
mechanism_summary: 2'-fucosyllactose (2'-FL) and lacto-N-neotetraose (LNnT); Bifidobacterium-specific prebiotic. Selectively expand Bifidobacteria; restore infant-like gut.
notes: Infant formula fortification widespread.

name: Galactooligosaccharides (GOS) / fructooligosaccharides (FOS)
mechanism_summary: Prebiotic fibers that selectively feed Bifidobacterium and Faecalibacterium; raise SCFA production.
notes: Grimaldi 2018 RCT positive in ASD.

name: Sodium butyrate (postbiotic)
mechanism_summary: Direct delivery of butyrate; bypasses fermentation. HDAC inhibitor, T-reg promoter, BBB protector. Autism animal models positive.
notes: Animal models stronger than human RCT base.

name: Vancomycin (oral, transient pre-FMT)
mechanism_summary: Non-absorbed antibiotic used short-term to clear pathogenic Clostridia before FMT or microbiome reset. NOT systemic antibiotic — gut-only.
notes: Sandler 2000 (transient improvement); Hazan 2024 pre-FMT.

name: Antifungal protocol (nystatin / fluconazole, targeted)
mechanism_summary: Targeted antifungal therapy for documented Candida overgrowth in autism subset. Empirically used in functional medicine; controversial mainstream.
notes: Less peer-reviewed; functional med tradition.

name: Phospholipid DHA (krill oil / salmon roe)
mechanism_summary: Phospholipid form of DHA; ~10x preferential brain accumulation vs triglyceride DHA. Patrick emphasizes for pregnancy/lactation and developmental windows. Werner 2011 demonstrated fold-higher brain DHA from phospholipid form.
dose_range: 500-2000 mg combined EPA+DHA in PL form/day
notes: Distinct from INT-0014 standard fish oil.

name: Tryptophan supplementation (with vitamin D)
mechanism_summary: Patrick & Ames recommendation: tryptophan + vitamin D co-supplementation to support brain serotonin synthesis. Tryptophan crosses BBB via large neutral amino acid transporter; vitamin D upregulates TPH2 to use it.
notes: Speculative as autism intervention; mechanistic prior strong.

name: Creatine monohydrate
mechanism_summary: Methyl donor (via creatine synthesis cost), brain energy buffer via phosphocreatine, supports cognitive function. Methylation protocol component (with choline + glycine).
notes: Robust safety; emerging cognitive evidence.

name: Time-restricted eating / 16:8 fasting
mechanism_summary: Daily eating window restriction (typically 8 hours). Activates AMPK, autophagy, metabolic flexibility. Less direct autism evidence; mechanistic prior via mTOR/AMPK axis.
notes: Pediatric application requires clinical caution.

name: HIIT (high-intensity interval training)
mechanism_summary: High-intensity intervals produce more lactate (cf. Brooks lactate shuttle), driving more BDNF crossing BBB. Patrick + Gibala emphasize for cognitive/neuroplasticity benefit. Distinct from steady-state aerobic (INT-0047).
notes: Pediatric adaptation needed; very modifiable.

name: Whole-body hyperthermia (clinical)
mechanism_summary: Medically supervised raising of core body temperature to ~38.5°C. Activates HSF1/HSP70, modulates immune response. Investigational for autism per fever-effect rationale.
notes: Distinct from home sauna (INT-0048); investigational.

name: Hyperbaric oxygen therapy (HBOT)
mechanism_summary: Pressurized oxygen (1.3-1.5 ATA) delivered via chamber. Hypothesized to address tissue hypoxia, reduce neuroinflammation, and modulate cerebral blood flow. Evidence mixed — Rossignol 2009 multicenter RCT (n=62) reported modest improvements, Granpeesheh 2010 trial null, meta-analyses suggest possible subset response.
notes: Genuinely contested; positive in subset, negative overall in some RCTs. Per spec §1.1 included with honest provenance.

name: Alpha-lipoic acid (ALA)
mechanism_summary: Mitochondrial cofactor and antioxidant; regenerates glutathione, vitamin C, vitamin E. Crosses BBB. Used in mitochondrial cocktails for autism subset.
notes: Adams 2018 multivitamin RCT included ALA.

name: D-ribose
mechanism_summary: Pentose sugar; ATP synthesis substrate via 5-phosphoribosyl-1-pyrophosphate (PRPP). Used for mitochondrial bioenergetic support in fibromyalgia, cardiac, and autism subsets.
notes: Limited autism RCT base; mitochondrial mechanism plausible.

name: Niacinamide / nicotinamide
mechanism_summary: Vitamin B3 form; NAD+ precursor (alternative to NMN/NR), PARP activity support, anti-inflammatory, mitochondrial biogenesis support.
notes: Bridges with NAD+ precursors INT-0068.

name: Preconception mitochondrial optimization combo (parental)
mechanism_summary: Combined preconception protocol for both prospective parents (starting 3-6 months pre-conception): CoQ10 (ubiquinol) + L-carnitine + alpha-lipoic acid + B-complex methylated forms + omega-3 phospholipid DHA + magnesium + zinc + selenium + vitamin D3 + iodine. Targets mitochondrial DNA quality of ova/sperm, methylation capacity, antioxidant pool, and epigenetic programming during gametogenesis.
notes: Combination first-class; targets HYP-0006 + HYP-0031 + HYP-0003 + HYP-0021 + HYP-0045 simultaneously.

name: Red light / photobiomodulation therapy (635 nm)
mechanism_summary: Transcranial red laser (635 nm, 5 min, 2x/week, 4 weeks) applied at skull base/temples. Leisman 2018 double-blind RCT (n=21-22) showed ~50% reductions in irritability, lethargy/social withdrawal, stereotypic behavior, hyperactivity, and total ABC-C score vs. placebo LED, with gains persisting. Mechanism: photobiomodulation activates cytochrome c oxidase, boosts ATP, reduces neuroinflammation.
dose_range: 635 nm laser, 5 min @ skull base + temples, 2x/week, 4 weeks
notes: Small but rigorous RCT; replication needed. Outdoctrination X clip referenced this study.

name: Vitamin A megadose (single 200,000 IU)
mechanism_summary: Guo 2018 pilot showed single 200,000 IU vitamin A in vitamin-A-deficient ASD children reduced symptoms and lowered serum serotonin (peripheral hyperserotonemia is documented in autism). Mechanism: RAR/RXR signaling, gut-serotonin modulation. Liver/cod-liver-oil dietary equivalent safer long-term. NOT routine — only with documented deficiency and clinician supervision (vit A is hepatotoxic at high chronic doses).
dose_range: Single 200,000 IU under clinician supervision, only if documented deficiency
notes: Pilot evidence; caution required — vitamin A toxicity is real.

name: Chelation therapy (DMSA / EDTA)
mechanism_summary: Pharmacological chelation of heavy metals (mercury, lead, aluminum) with DMSA, DMPS, or EDTA. Adams 2009 small DMSA pilot showed urinary metal output but mixed clinical effect. James 2015 Cochrane review concluded INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE of benefit on core ASD symptoms and documented significant risks including renal injury and death (one fatal hypocalcemia case from disodium-EDTA confusion in 2005). AAP and FDA warn against chelation for ASD outside proven heavy-metal poisoning. Per spec §1.1 added with negative-tilting evidence; NOT recommended.
dose_range: DMSA 10 mg/kg/dose protocols studied; NOT recommended
notes: James 2015 Cochrane: insufficient evidence + harm. Pediatric_safe=no due to documented fatalities. Status active for tracking; effective use is contraindicated outside true heavy-metal poisoning.

name: Cod liver oil (fermented preferred)
mechanism_summary: Traditional whole-food source of vitamin D3 + DHA/EPA omega-3 + vitamin A (retinol) + (in fermented form) vitamin K2. Hits multiple convergent pathways: vitamin D → TPH2 / brain serotonin synthesis (MEC-0029); DHA → membrane phospholipid composition (MEC-0031); vitamin A → developmental morphogenesis. Pre-conception and pregnancy use historically associated with lower neurodevelopmental issues in offspring (Norwegian MoBa cohort).
dose_range: 1 tsp/day (≈1g) standard; fermented cod liver oil 0.5-1 tsp/day per Weston A. Price Foundation; vitamin A content ~3000-10000 IU varies by product
notes: Whole-food traditional supplement covering 3-4 high-leverage preventive nutrients in one product. Pre-conception use (both parents) recommended. Avoid synthetic vitamin A megadose products; cod liver oil's natural retinol form is well tolerated.
targets_legacy: vitamin D receptor; TPH2; DHA/EPA membrane incorporation; vitamin A signaling; vitamin K2

name: L-glutamine (free amino acid)
mechanism_summary: Conditionally essential amino acid; primary fuel for enterocytes; supports intestinal barrier integrity. Down-regulates zonulin release, supports tight junction proteins (occludin, claudin-1, ZO-1). Hits gut-brain axis (MEC-0008) and LPS endotoxemia / leaky gut (MEC-0022). Component of GFCF gut-healing protocols; member of COM-0003.
dose_range: 5-15 g/day in divided doses, away from meals
notes: Caution: glutamine is a glutamate precursor; in children with documented GABA/glutamate imbalance phenotype (PHE-0007) start at lower doses and monitor for agitation/anxiety. Most autism-relevant for the GI/microbiome subtype (PHE-0004).
targets_legacy: intestinal tight junctions; zonulin; enterocyte energy metabolism; glutathione precursor (Gly-Cys-Glu)

name: Stem cell therapy (UC-MSC / cord blood / cord tissue MSC, IV infusion)
notes: Stem cell therapy in autism is mechanistically grounded in immune modulation, paracrine signaling (exosomes carrying neurotrophic factors and regulatory RNAs), mitochondrial transfer (MSCs can transfer mitochondria to neighboring stressed cells), and reduction of microglial activation. Despite Phase 2 RCT (Dawson 2020) being formally null on the primary outcome in unstratified analysis, subgroup analysis showed signal in the non-anxious / normal-IQ subset and in the inflammatory subgroup — exactly the effect-heterogeneity pattern CLAUDE.md §9 predicts. Strength_score reflects mixed evidence weighted by mechanism plausibility, safety profile, and subgroup-stratified response signal.

name: Cromolyn sodium (oral)
mechanism_summary: Mast cell stabilizer; pre-meal for GI mast cell stabilization
notes: MCAS/PHE-0008 candidate per Theoharides framework

name: Ketotifen
mechanism_summary: Combined H1 antihistamine + mast cell stabilizer
notes: MCAS protocol; compounded available

name: Luteolin (BBB-crossing formulation)
mechanism_summary: BBB-crossing bioflavonoid; stabilizes mast cells AND microglia
notes: Per Theoharides framework. NeuroProtek and similar formulations.

name: H1+H2 antihistamine combination
mechanism_summary: Cetirizine/fexofenadine (H1) + famotidine (H2) — foundational MCAS
notes: Foundational MCAS protocol. Loratadine alternative for daytime.

name: Nystatin (oral non-systemic)
mechanism_summary: Polyene antifungal; non-absorbable; targets gut Candida
notes: First-line for elevated arabinose / Candida overgrowth

name: Caprylic acid (C8 fatty acid)
mechanism_summary: Medium-chain fatty acid antifungal

name: Undecylenic acid
mechanism_summary: Antifungal; complementary to nystatin

name: Biofilm disruptors (lumbrokinase / NAC / lactoferrin)
mechanism_summary: Biofilm-degrading enzymes + chelators; pre-antifungal/antimicrobial

name: Vancomycin (oral, non-absorbable)
mechanism_summary: Targets Clostridia overgrowth; non-systemic
notes: For elevated HPHPA / Clostridia (Sandler 2000 case series)

name: Saccharomyces boulardii
mechanism_summary: Probiotic yeast; targets C. diff + Clostridia

name: Berberine
mechanism_summary: Plant alkaloid; antimicrobial + AMPK activation

name: Methyl-B12 (subcutaneous Neubrander protocol)
mechanism_summary: Methylcobalamin SC; bypasses B12 metabolic blocks
notes: Per Neubrander_James protocol. Methylated folate often co-administered.

name: SAM-e (S-adenosylmethionine)
mechanism_summary: Direct methyl donor; bypasses cycle defects
notes: Undermethylators per Walsh; not for overmethylators or bipolar-spectrum

name: Betaine / TMG (trimethylglycine)
mechanism_summary: BHMT pathway methyl donor; lowers homocysteine
notes: Especially when homocysteine elevated

name: PQQ (pyrroloquinoline quinone)
mechanism_summary: Mitochondrial biogenesis promoter
notes: Pyrroloquinoline quinone — promotes mitochondrial biogenesis (creates new mitochondria, not just supports existing function). Generally well-tolerated in pediatric supplementation; minimal published autism-specific clinical trials. Mechanism via PGC-1α activation.

name: MitoQ (mito-targeted CoQ10)
mechanism_summary: Mitochondria-targeted CoQ10 analog
notes: Mitochondria-targeted CoQ10 analog covalently linked to triphenylphosphonium cation, allowing 100-1000x mitochondrial enrichment vs standard CoQ10. Preclinical neurodegenerative + autism research; clinical use emerging. Lower per-mg dosing than standard CoQ10.

name: NAD+ precursors (NMN/NR)
mechanism_summary: Boost cellular NAD+; sirtuin pathway support
notes: Regulatory note: FDA reclassified NMN as drug for NDI purposes (2022); NR remains as supplement.

name: Methylene blue (low-dose)
mechanism_summary: Alternative electron carrier; methemoglobin caution
notes: Emerging; methemoglobinemia risk; G6PD contraindication

name: Bumetanide (NKCC1 inhibitor for autism)
mechanism_summary: NKCC1 inhibitor; forces GABA developmental switch in chloride-imbalance subgroup
notes: Per Ben-Ari framework; Phase 3 mixed but subgroup-positive.

name: Suramin (low-dose, antipurinergic)
mechanism_summary: Antipurinergic; Naviaux CDR framework
notes: Naviaux 2017 PMID 28695149 phase I/II; toxicity limits broad use

name: Pioglitazone (PPAR-γ agonist)
mechanism_summary: PPAR-γ agonist; anti-inflammatory; insulin-sensitizer
notes: Emerging neuroinflammation use; weight gain caution

name: Minocycline (microglial modulator)
mechanism_summary: Tetracycline with microglial-modulating activity
notes: Anti-inflammatory; long-term skin pigmentation caution

name: Allopregnanolone / ganaxolone
mechanism_summary: Endogenous neurosteroid; positive GABA-A modulator
notes: Calming; emerging research in epileptic ASD

name: Trehalose (autophagy promoter)
mechanism_summary: Disaccharide; promotes autophagy and mitophagy
notes: Disaccharide that promotes autophagy / mitophagy via mTOR-independent pathway. Clearance of damaged mitochondria + protein aggregates. Emerging research in neurodegenerative + neurodevelopmental contexts. Generally well-tolerated; some GI tolerance issues at higher doses.

name: Lovastatin (low-dose)
mechanism_summary: Statin with FXR / cholesterol effects; preclinical Fragile X work
dose_range: contested; not for general autism use
mainstream_consensus_position: Statins (HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors including lovastatin) are FDA-approved and well-established for cardiovascular indications (hyperlipidemia, atherosclerotic disease). Their use in autism specifically — investigated by Frye et al for FXR / Ras-pathway modulation in subset cohorts — is investigational with limited published clinical evidence and is not a mainstream pediatric-neurology recommendation. Off-label use should be discussed with clinician oversight; CSRS in this atlas reflects mechanistic rationale + small published series, not population-validated efficacy.
notes: Preclinical Fragile X work only; mitochondrially toxic + CoQ10 depleting. Not for general autism use. Status: contested.

name: Valacyclovir / acyclovir (HHV-6/EBV reactivation)
mechanism_summary: Antiviral for chronic herpesvirus reactivation

name: Vasopressin (intranasal)
mechanism_summary: Neuropeptide; social bonding mechanism (complementary to oxytocin)

name: IGF-1 (mecasermin)
mechanism_summary: Insulin-like growth factor 1; SHANK3-stratified intervention
notes: Per Hollander framework for Phelan-McDermid syndrome; off-label autism

name: Antimicrobial peptides (cathelicidin/LL-37)
mechanism_summary: Endogenous AMP; vitamin-D-driven; first-line innate immunity

name: Humanin (mito-derived peptide)
mechanism_summary: Mitochondrial 16S-rRNA encoded peptide; anti-apoptotic + neuroprotective

name: MOTS-c (mito-derived peptide)
mechanism_summary: Mitochondrial 12S-rRNA peptide; metabolic + exercise-mimetic effects

name: Pregnenolone (low-dose)
mechanism_summary: Master neurosteroid; precursor for cortisol/DHEA/allopregnanolone
notes: Use cautiously in children; existing INT-0069 covers

name: Vitamin D high-dose (deficiency-correction)
mechanism_summary: Cholecalciferol; immune/serotonin/methylation effects
notes: Foundational; calibrated to BIO-0165

name: L-carnosine (oral dipeptide)
mechanism_summary: Beta-alanyl-L-histidine dipeptide. GABA modulation via homocarnosine pathway; possible anticonvulsant + frontal/temporal cortex protective effects. Used by Chez 2002 J Child Neurol RCT (PMID 12585724) at 800 mg/day in autism.
mainstream_consensus_position: Small pediatric RCT (Chez 2002, n=31) showed significant improvement on Gilliam ARS + Receptive Vocabulary; not replicated at scale.
notes: Added 2026-05-09 to fix rrc_005 cohort entry. Distinct molecule from L-carnitine (INT-0011). FRM-0047 reparented to this entry.

name: Aripiprazole
mechanism_summary: Atypical antipsychotic; partial dopamine D2 agonist + serotonin 5-HT1A agonist + 5-HT2A antagonist. FDA-approved for autism-associated irritability (children 6-17) based on Owen 2009 + Marcus 2009 RCTs.
mainstream_consensus_position: FDA-approved for pediatric autism irritability. Owen 2009 (PMID 19948625): 52% responder rate on ABC-Irritability ≥25% reduction + CGI-I 1 or 2. Metabolic side effects + weight gain require monitoring.
notes: Added 2026-05-09 to fix rrc_008 cohort entry. Mainstream pharma intervention; population-average effect rather than biomarker-stratified subset. Used as cohort calibration baseline for non-stratified RCTs.

name: Vitamin/mineral multinutrient supplement (Adams 2011 formulation)
mechanism_summary: Broad-spectrum vitamin + mineral + antioxidant + amino-acid + essential-fatty-acid multinutrient. Adams 2011 BMC Pediatrics RCT formulation (PMID 22151477). Targets multiple nutritional deficiencies commonly observed in autism cohorts.
mainstream_consensus_position: Small RCT (n=104, Adams 2011): continuous outcomes only (PGI-R Average Change p=0.008); no dichotomized responder rate published. Bonus regression analysis: biotin + vitamin K were strongest baseline predictors of clinical response.
notes: Added 2026-05-09 to fix rrc_011 cohort entry. Adams formulation; not a single commercial product.

name: IVIG (intravenous immunoglobulin)
mechanism_summary: Pooled-donor immunoglobulin G concentrate. Anti-inflammatory + immunomodulatory in autoimmune neuropsychiatric conditions. Used in PANS/PANDAS subset of autism per Frankovich 2017 PANS Research Consortium guidelines.
dose_range: 1.0-2.0 g/kg per infusion course; cycle schedule varies
mainstream_consensus_position: Perlmutter 1999 PANDAS RCT showed OC symptom reduction. Williams 2016 PANDAS RCT did not reach binary statistical significance; open-label IVIG ~50% OCD severity reduction. Frankovich 2017 guidelines list IVIG as preferred treatment for moderate-to-severe PANS.
notes: Added 2026-05-09 to fix rrc_013 cohort entry. Specialist intervention; thrombotic + aseptic-meningitis risk. PANS-overlap subset of autism.

name: L-theanine
mechanism_summary: γ-glutamylethylamide; non-protein amino acid from Camellia sinensis. Modulates glutamate/GABA balance; induces alpha-wave EEG activity; anxiolytic without sedation.
mainstream_consensus_position: Pediatric ADHD + anxiety small RCT support; autism-specific evidence is small + indirect.
notes: Added 2026-05-09 to ground COM-0016 (Calm-axis stack) and COM-0018 (Sleep-architecture stack), both of which named L-theanine as a member but had no atlas-resident INT entry.
